edges.description: Cochlear basement membrane dysfunction produces progressive sensorineural hearing impairment.
Lens-capsule basement membrane weakness produces anterior lenticonus.
Retinal basement membrane defects produce fleck retinopathy.
Corneal basement membrane defects produce corneal opacities.
Progressive GBM thickening, thinning, splitting, and basket-weave remodeling produces lamellation.
Structural GBM compromise causes persistent glomerular hematuria.
Progressive GBM permeability and podocyte injury cause proteinuria.
Segmental glomerular scarring manifests histologically as FSGS.
Chronic tubulointerstitial fibrosis drives progressive nephron loss and renal failure.
Progressive renal damage contributes to hypertension that requires monitoring and treatment.

nodes.description: Conical protrusion of the anterior lens surface, pathognomonic for Alport syndrome when present. Occurs predominantly in X-linked males. Results from defective type IV collagen in the lens capsule.

evidence.reference_title: Ocular features in Alport syndrome: pathogenesis and clinical significance.
evidence.snippet: Lenticonus and central fleck retinopathy strongly suggest the diagnosis of Alport syndrome and are associated with renal failure before the age of 30 years, in males with X-linked disease.

evidence.reference_title: Alport Syndrome: A Comprehensive Review.
evidence.snippet: changes in the ocular lens, named anterior lenticonus, can occur, causing vision problems.

nodes.description: Loss of the collagen IV alpha3-alpha4-alpha5 network from cochlear basement membranes disrupts inner-ear structural integrity, producing progressive high-frequency sensorineural hearing loss.

evidence.reference_title: Alport Syndrome: A Comprehensive Review.
evidence.snippet: In some cases of Alport syndrome, the abnormal collagen can also affect the cochlea in the inner ear, leading to sensorineural hearing loss.

nodes.description: Corneal opacities can occur due to defective type IV collagen in the corneal basement membrane. Additional corneal findings include posterior polymorphous corneal dystrophy and recurrent corneal erosions.

evidence.reference_title: Ocular features in Alport syndrome: pathogenesis and clinical significance.
evidence.snippet: are associated with corneal opacities, anterior lenticonus, fleck retinopathy, and temporal retinal thinning.

nodes.description: Mutations in COL4A3, COL4A4, or COL4A5 disrupt the alpha3-alpha4-alpha5(IV) collagen network, which is the predominant collagen IV isoform in the mature glomerular basement membrane, cochlea, and lens capsule. The defective network is replaced by the embryonic alpha1-alpha1-alpha2(IV) network, which is more susceptible to proteolytic degradation and cannot maintain normal structural integrity.

evidence.reference_title: Alport Syndrome: Achieving Early Diagnosis and Treatment.
evidence.snippet: Alport syndrome is a genetically and phenotypically heterogeneous disorder of glomerular, cochlear, and ocular basement membranes resulting from mutations in the collagen IV genes COL4A3, COL4A4, and COL4A5.

evidence.reference_title: Ocular features in Alport syndrome: pathogenesis and clinical significance.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in the COL4A5 (X-linked), or COL4A3 and COL4A4 (autosomal recessive) genes result in absence of the collagen IV α3α4α5 network from the basement membranes of the cornea, lens capsule, and retina

nodes.description: White or yellow dots and flecks in the perimacular region, a characteristic ocular finding in Alport syndrome. Does not typically affect visual acuity.

evidence.reference_title: Ocular features in Alport syndrome: pathogenesis and clinical significance.
evidence.snippet: are associated with corneal opacities, anterior lenticonus, fleck retinopathy, and temporal retinal thinning.

nodes.description: Secondary FSGS develops as podocyte loss and GBM deterioration progress. This is a histopathologic finding on renal biopsy.

evidence.reference_title: Synaptopodin deficiency exacerbates kidney disease in a mouse model of Alport syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Lack of Synpo in Col4a5-/Y, Col4a5-/-, or Col4a5+/- Alport mice led to the acceleration of disease progression, including more severe proteinuria and glomerulosclerosis.

nodes.description: Characteristic ultrastructural finding on electron microscopy showing thickening, thinning, splitting, and basket-weave lamellation of the glomerular basement membrane. This is the hallmark histopathologic feature of Alport syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Synaptopodin deficiency exacerbates kidney disease in a mouse model of Alport syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We used collagen type IV-α5 (Col4a5) mutant mice, which model X-linked AS, showing glomerular basement membrane (GBM) abnormalities, eventual foot process effacement, and progression to end-stage kidney disease.

nodes.description: The abnormal GBM undergoes progressive thickening, thinning, splitting, and lamellation (characteristic basket-weave appearance on electron microscopy). The structurally compromised GBM becomes increasingly permeable, leading to progressive proteinuria and declining renal function.

evidence.reference_title: Synaptopodin deficiency exacerbates kidney disease in a mouse model of Alport syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We used collagen type IV-α5 (Col4a5) mutant mice, which model X-linked AS, showing glomerular basement membrane (GBM) abnormalities, eventual foot process effacement, and progression to end-stage kidney disease.

nodes.description: As podocytes are lost and the GBM degenerates, glomeruli undergo segmental and then global sclerosis. Mesangial expansion and increased extracellular matrix deposition contribute to progressive obliteration of glomerular capillaries.

evidence.reference_title: Synaptopodin deficiency exacerbates kidney disease in a mouse model of Alport syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Lack of Synpo in Col4a5-/Y, Col4a5-/-, or Col4a5+/- Alport mice led to the acceleration of disease progression, including more severe proteinuria and glomerulosclerosis.

nodes.description: Hypertension develops as renal function declines, contributing to further kidney damage. Blood pressure control is an important aspect of management.

evidence.reference_title: Alport Syndrome: A Comprehensive Review.
evidence.snippet: Regular monitoring of kidney function and blood pressure, along with medications to control hypertension, are crucial aspects of the management plan.

nodes.description: Persistent microscopic hematuria is the earliest and most consistent finding in Alport syndrome, typically present from early childhood in affected males and most carrier females.

evidence.reference_title: A multicenter, randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind phase 3 trial with open-arm comparison indicates safety and efficacy of nephroprotective therapy with ramipril in children with Alport's syndrome.
evidence.snippet: screening programs for glomerular hematuria in children and young adults could benefit from inclusion of genetic testing for Alport-related gene-variants.

nodes.description: Absence of the collagen IV alpha3-alpha4-alpha5 network from ocular basement membranes weakens the lens capsule, cornea, and retinal basement membranes, producing characteristic Alport ocular lesions.

evidence.reference_title: Ocular features in Alport syndrome: pathogenesis and clinical significance.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in the COL4A5 (X-linked), or COL4A3 and COL4A4 (autosomal recessive) genes result in absence of the collagen IV α3α4α5 network from the basement membranes of the cornea, lens capsule, and retina

nodes.description: Podocytes, which are normally anchored to the GBM, undergo foot process effacement and progressive detachment as the GBM deteriorates. Podocyte loss drives glomerulosclerosis and is a key determinant of the rate of progression to end-stage renal disease.

evidence.reference_title: Synaptopodin deficiency exacerbates kidney disease in a mouse model of Alport syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We speculate that this is mechanistically associated with enhanced loss of podocytes due to easier detachment from the GBM.

nodes.description: Progressive decline in renal function leading to end-stage renal disease (ESRD). In X-linked males, ESRD typically occurs by the second to third decade; in autosomal recessive cases, by the third decade; in autosomal dominant cases, often later.

evidence.reference_title: Meta-analysis of genotype-phenotype correlation in X-linked Alport syndrome: impact on clinical counselling.
evidence.snippet: large rearrangements, frame shift, nonsense, and splice donor mutations had a mean ESRF age of 19.8+/-5.7 years

evidence.reference_title: A multicenter, randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind phase 3 trial with open-arm comparison indicates safety and efficacy of nephroprotective therapy with ramipril in children with Alport's syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Children with Alport syndrome develop renal failure early in life.

nodes.description: Progressive proteinuria develops as GBM deterioration worsens, typically appearing in childhood to adolescence in X-linked males. Initially mild, it may progress to nephrotic-range proteinuria.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical practice recommendations for the diagnosis and management of Alport syndrome in children, adolescents, and young adults-an update for 2020.
evidence.snippet: We recommended delaying the initiation of angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibition until the onset of overt proteinuria or, in some cases, microalbuminuria.

evidence.reference_title: A multicenter, randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind phase 3 trial with open-arm comparison indicates safety and efficacy of nephroprotective therapy with ramipril in children with Alport's syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Ramipril decreased the risk of disease progression by almost half (hazard ratio 0.51 (0.12-2.20)), diminished the slope of albuminuria progression and the decline in glomerular filtration.

nodes.description: Bilateral high-frequency sensorineural hearing loss, typically developing in late childhood to adolescence. More common and severe in X-linked males. Not present at birth but progressive, eventually affecting conversational frequencies.

evidence.reference_title: Alport Syndrome: A Comprehensive Review.
evidence.snippet: In some cases of Alport syndrome, the abnormal collagen can also affect the cochlea in the inner ear, leading to sensorineural hearing loss.

evidence.reference_title: Meta-analysis of genotype-phenotype correlation in X-linked Alport syndrome: impact on clinical counselling.
evidence.snippet: Alport syndrome (AS) is a hereditary nephropathy characterized by progressive renal failure, hearing loss and ocular lesions.

nodes.description: Secondary tubulointerstitial fibrosis develops due to proteinuria-induced tubular injury and inflammatory infiltration. Chronic tubular damage and interstitial inflammation drive progressive nephron loss, ultimately leading to end-stage renal disease.

evidence.reference_title: From RAAS blockade to regenerative medicine: evolving treatment strategies in Alport syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Emphasis is placed on the molecular mechanisms underlying podocyte injury and fibrosis, recent preclinical findings, and ongoing clinical trials that may shift future therapeutic paradigms.
